title: lobelia burmanniana
intro: Lobelia burmanniana, also known as Japanese lobelia or Indian tobacco, is a species of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including bronchitis, asthma, and rheumatism, due to its health benefits such as reducing inflammation and relieving respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Lobelia burmanniana are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
